headlines.title: INSURANCE MEN TO SEEK REPEAL OF LAWS
headlines.subtitle: Meeting Here Will Ask Gov- ernor to Submit Measures to Special Session.
headlines.fullText: Insurance men from Fort Worth and Dallas, state or district representa- tives of various companies, were pres- ent at a meeting of business men which opened in the Chamber of Commerce rooms at 2 o'clock PTbody afternoon to discuss the "technicality bill" and the "anticoinsurance law" passed by The meeting was called as a result and the insurance men present were favorable to a resolution asking that Governor Colquitt submit both mens- ures to the special session which opens July 21. for repeal The technicality bill. the Insurance men point out, practically volds every condition of the insurance contract. and if upheld by the courts will make the policy contract covering on per- sobal property a liquidated demand whenever a fire occurs. Argon and fraudulent fires will any increase in losses will mean an increase in raten sooner or later, they The coinsurance clause, prohibited by the second measure they oppose. the insurance men suy is a sort of in- surance equalization board. The in- surance premium, they claim, is a sort of tax, and protection should be meas- ured according to the amount of the Being denied by law the right to equalize the Insurance tax, the com- un who objects to carrying enough insurance to pay his part of the all citizens in order to make up the deficit of the few. The Inw is class legislation, they claim, because it per- mits certain classes of property own- to postmet fer ceinsurance and de- nies the right to others. Among the Dallas insurance men at- tending the meeting were: A. R. Phil- lips, Gross R. Scruggs, T. L. Lauve, Wirt Lacake, Charles F. Thomas and John M. Thomas.
